ogTitle: Boho South vs Boigbeat: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Boho South and Boigbeat.
heading: Boho South vs Boigbeat — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Boho South, Victoria and Boigbeat, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Boho South has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Boigbeat (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Boho South or Boigbeat safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Boho South has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Boigbeat scores 20/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Boho South scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. Boigbeat scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Boho South or Boigbeat?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Boho South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
